description: Simplify Auth - Remove Google sign-in and phone OTP verification, make login full-page, single-form passenger registration with email-only OTP, prominent signup CTA, and login UX polish. Full PRD: tasks/prd-simplify-auth-login.md

userStories.title: Remove Google sign-in from the client
userStories.description: As a passenger, I should not see a 'Continue with Google' option that doesn't work, so that I'm never dead-ended.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove .btn-google button from app.html (lines ~75-78)
Remove #google-phone-card markup from app.html (lines ~273-306)

Remove client JS: doGoogleSignIn, googlePhoneSendOtp, googlePhoneVerify, googlePhoneResendOtp, googlePending state, and google_success/google_error/google_pending URL-param handling from js/app/02-auth.js AND js/app.bundle.js (bundle is a concatenation of js/app/*.js modules - keep both in sync, check scripts/ for a build script first)
Remove 'google-phone-card' and 'google-phone-error' entries from the auth card/error id arrays in the JS
Remove .btn-google CSS rules from css/app.css (lines ~474-490)
Bump the ?v= cache-buster on the app.bundle.js script tag in app.html
No console errors on page load
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: ROLLED BACK 2026-07-05: Google sign-in is now working in production. Commit reverted (see d2f1d95/1353e4a). Google sign-in code must be KEPT. Do not re-implement this story. passes=true only to prevent re-execution.

userStories.title: Remove Google OAuth server routes
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want dead Google OAuth endpoints removed so the maintenance surface shrinks.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove GET /api/auth/google, GET /api/auth/google/callback, POST /api/auth/google/send-phone-otp and any other google routes from src/auth-routes.js
Delete src/google-auth.js and remove all imports/references to it
Remove GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET entries from .env.example
Server starts without errors (node server.js boots or npm start)
Existing email/password login endpoint still responds (POST /api/auth/login returns 400/401 for bad creds, not 500)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ROLLED BACK 2026-07-05: Google sign-in is now working in production. Commit reverted (see d2f1d95/1353e4a). Google sign-in code must be KEPT. Do not re-implement this story. passes=true only to prevent re-execution.

userStories.title: Server accepts email-only signup (remove phone OTP endpoints)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want signup endpoints to create accounts with email OTP alone so phone verification is fully retired.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/role-signup.js, verify-signup creates the account when the email OTP is valid; phone and phoneOtp params are ignored (not rejected) if sent
Remove the send-phone-otp endpoint registration and its handler from src/role-signup.js
Remove the phone-uniqueness check from signup-request (no phone collected at signup)
Remove now-unused createAndSendMobileOtp/normalizePhone imports from src/role-signup.js if nothing else in the file uses them
New accounts persist with phone NULL; existing accounts with phone numbers are unaffected
Manual API test: signup-request with name/email/password sends email OTP, verify-signup with correct emailOtp creates account, login with new credentials succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Single-form passenger registration with inline email OTP
userStories.description: As a new passenger, I want to register on one page with just my email verified so signup takes under a minute.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace #register-passenger-card and #register-verify-card in app.html with ONE card containing: Full Name, Gender (optional select), Age (optional number), Email, Password, and inline email OTP verification
Remove the step indicator (auth-steps markup: '1 Personal details / 2 Verify')
Flow: fill fields, click 'Send verification code', OTP input appears inline on same card, enter 6-digit code, click 'Create account'
Remove all phone fields, phone OTP inputs, and regSendPhoneOtp/regResendPhoneOtp client code from js/app/02-auth.js and js/app.bundle.js
Client-side validation before sending OTP: name non-empty, valid email format, password >= 6 chars; errors shown inline
Resend-code button uses the existing 30s cooldown pattern with countdown hint
'Already have an account? Sign in' link at the bottom of the card
Bump the ?v= cache-buster on app.bundle.js script tag
Full round-trip works: register, receive OTP, verify, land in booking view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Full-page login layout (remove right hero panel)
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want a focused full-page sign-in screen so login feels clean and direct.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove .login-right markup (dark hero with 'Sky is no longer the limit') from app.html
#login-view renders logo, tagline, and auth card centered horizontally and vertically on the full viewport
Remove .login-right*, .login-features, .login-feature* CSS from css/app.css; restyle .login-left to fill the page; remove the now-dead '.login-right { display: none; }' mobile rule (~line 2069)
Auth card max-width approximately 420px with a subtle non-distracting page background
No horizontal scroll at 360px, 768px, and 1440px viewport widths
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Prominent 'Create passenger account' button on login card
userStories.description: As a new passenger, I want an obvious signup button on the login page so I don't hunt for a small text link.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the 'Don't have an account? Register' text-link row (#login-register-row) with: a thin divider labeled 'New to IraGo?' followed by a full-width secondary button labeled 'Create passenger account'
Button styled as secondary (outlined, similar to .btn-profile-outline), visually distinct from the primary Sign In button
Clicking the button navigates to /app.html?register=1
Button and divider are hidden when the Operator or Admin role tab is selected (switchLoginRole hides them, same as current register-row behavior)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: register=1 deep link opens the registration form
userStories.description: As a passenger following a marketing link, I want /app.html?register=1 to land directly on the create-account form.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading /app.html?register=1 while logged out shows the single registration card immediately
Loading /app.html?register=1 while logged in goes to the normal logged-in view (preserve current behavior)
goToSignupPortal() and the landing page signupUrl ('/app.html?register=1') still work
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Login card UX polish
userStories.description: As a passenger, I want a smoother, clearer sign-in experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Password fields on login AND registration cards have a show/hide (eye icon) toggle
Sign In button shows a loading state ('Signing in...') and is disabled while the login request is in flight; same pattern for registration submit buttons
Failed login shows friendly inline error 'Incorrect email or password' (map server error text) without clearing the email field
Email field autofocuses when the login card is shown; name field autofocuses when the registration card is shown
Invalid email format and password shorter than 6 chars show per-field inline hints on blur, before submit
'Forgot password?' link moved to the right of the Password label (right-aligned in the label row), removed from below the submit button
Role switcher (Passenger/Operator/Admin) still works and sits visually de-emphasized at the bottom of the card
Bump the ?v= cache-buster on app.bundle.js script tag
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Clean up phone verification remnants in profile modal
userStories.description: As a signed-in passenger, I should not be pushed to verify a phone number the platform no longer verifies.
acceptanceCriteria: First grep operator/admin/dispatch render code for passenger phone display: if passenger phone is shown anywhere, reduce #profile-phone-block to an optional plain phone-number field (no OTP flow); otherwise remove #profile-phone-block entirely
Remove doProfilePhoneSendOtp/doProfilePhoneVerifyOtp/doProfilePhoneResendOtp OTP flow code from the client JS (js/app/*.js and js/app.bundle.js)
No 'Verify phone' CTA remains anywhere in the passenger UI
Record the chosen option (remove vs plain field) and the grep evidence in notes
Bump the ?v= cache-buster on app.bundle.js script tag
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Removed #profile-phone-block entirely (grep confirmed no operator/admin/dispatch code displays passenger phone). Kept Google phone card (.btn-google, #google-phone-card) per rollback decision.
